2606.15874.title: LLM-as-Code Agentic Programming for Agent Harness
2606.15874.paper_summary: Every major LLM agent framework gives the LLM the role of orchestrator; the model decides what to do next, when to call tools, and when to stop. We argue that token explosion, control-flow hallucination, and unreliable completion are not implementation bugs but architectural consequences of assigning the deterministic work of looping, branching, and sequencing to a probabilistic system. A better prompt or a stronger model cannot guarantee the reliability of the LLM agent. We therefore propose Agentic Programming, in which the program governs all control flow, and the LLM is itself part of it, an adaptive component we call LLM-as-Code and invoke only where a task calls for reasoning or generation. Within each call the model keeps full flexibility, but it cannot alter the program's execution path. With control in the program, the LLM's context is built from the execution history's call tree and forms a directed acyclic graph (DAG). Each call's context length is then determined by its call depth rather than by accumulation over steps. A case study of computer-use agents shows that the design is practical, not just a theoretical stance, substantially improving the stability of long visual operation sequences.
2606.15874.paper_summary_zh: 每個主要的 LLM 代理框架都賦予 LLM 指揮者的角色；模型決定接下來該做什麼、何時調用工具以及何時停止。我們認為，令牌爆炸、控制流幻覺和不可靠的完成並不是實現錯誤，而是將循環、分支和序列的確定性工作分配給概率系統的架構後果。更好的提示或更強大的模型無法保證 LLM 代理的可靠性。因此，我們提出了代理編程，其中程序控制所有控制流，而 LLM 自身是其中的一部分，我們稱之為 LLM-as-Code，僅在任務需要推理或生成時調用。在每次調用中，模型保持完全的靈活性，但不能改變程序的執行路徑。通過控制程序，LLM 的上下文是從執行歷史的調用樹構建的，並形成一個有向無環圖（DAG）。每次調用的上下文長度由其調用深度決定，而不是通過步驟的累積。對計算機使用代理的案例研究顯示，這一設計是實用的，而不僅僅是一種理論立場，顯著提高了長視覺操作序列的穩定性。
2606.15874.author: Junjia Qi et.al.
2606.15874.authors: Junjia Qi, Zichuan Fu, Jingtong Gao, Wenlin Zhang, Hanyu Yan, Xian Wu, Xiangyu Zhao
